title: Chat messages broadcast & stored unsanitized (sanitizeChatMessage never wired in)
detail: sendChat() builds `const chatMessage = { ... message, ... }` from the raw string and both stores it locally (store.addChatMessage at line 608) and broadcasts it to peers (lines 611-614) with no sanitization. The matching receive path at line 354-355 `case 'CHAT': store.addChatMessage(message.payload);` stores raw peer-supplied content. sanitizeChatMessage() (sanitize.ts:178, strips \x00-\x1F\x7F control bytes, trims, slices to 500, HTML-encodes) exists and is exported but has ZERO callers. The UI maxLength=200 (MultiplayerChat.tsx:153) is a local-input-only attribute; untrusted remote peers can send arbitrarily long messages with control characters straight into the store. React text interpolation ({msg.message}) blunts direct-DOM XSS, so this is defense-in-depth + DoS/control-char hygiene rather than active script execution.
fix: In sendChat(), sanitize before building chatMessage: `const clean = sanitizeChatMessage(message); if (!clean) return; ... message: clean`. In handleMessage() CHAT case, sanitize on receive: `store.addChatMessage({ ...message.payload, message: sanitizeChatMessage(message.payload.message) });`. Import sanitizeChatMessage from '../utils'.
_area: L2 Security · Key handling + sanitize wiring + scaffolding

title: Player names from peer metadata stored unsanitized (sanitizePlayerName never wired in)
detail: handlePeerConnected reads `const playerName = metadata?.playerName || 'Player';` (line 141) from untrusted PeerJS connection metadata and stores it via store.addRemotePlayer/addPeer (lines 175-193) and re-broadcasts it (lines 228-238) with no sanitization. The PLAYER_JOIN receive handler does the same: `name: message.payload.name` (line 291). sanitizePlayerName() (sanitize.ts:162, caps to 20 chars, alnum/_/-/space only) exists and is exported but has ZERO callers. A malicious peer can inject arbitrary-length names with control chars that propagate to all clients and render in the chat/lobby UI.
fix: Wrap untrusted names: at line 141 `const playerName = sanitizePlayerName(metadata?.playerName) || 'Player';` and at line 291 `name: sanitizePlayerName(message.payload.name) || 'Player'`. Import sanitizePlayerName from '../utils'.
_area: L2 Security · Key handling + sanitize wiring + scaffolding

title: Local player name & room code entered without sanitizePlayerName/sanitizeRoomCode
detail: Name entry uses only `.trim()`: `setLocalPlayerName(playerName.trim())` and `manager.hostRoom(playerName.trim())` (lines 63-65), `manager.joinRoom(joinCode.trim().toUpperCase(), playerName.trim())` (line 89). Same pattern in MultiplayerPanel.tsx:94-96,118-120. sanitizePlayerName() (max 20, charset-restricted) and sanitizeRoomCode() (sanitize.ts:203, exactly 6 uppercase alnum) exist and are exported but unused at these entry points. Names of arbitrary length/charset enter the store and get broadcast to peers.
fix: Replace `.trim()` with `sanitizePlayerName(playerName)` at the setLocalPlayerName/host/join call sites in both MultiplayerLobby.tsx and MultiplayerPanel.tsx, and gate join with `const code = sanitizeRoomCode(joinCode); if (!code) return;`. Import from '@/utils'. Two-file change but each edit is intra-file.
_area: L2 Security · Key handling + sanitize wiring + scaffolding

title: Gemini API key persisted in plaintext localStorage with no user warning
detail: partialize persists the raw key: `partialize: (state) => ({ aiMode: state.aiMode, geminiApiKey: state.geminiApiKey, managementGenerosity: state.managementGenerosity })` under store name 'millos-ai-config' (line 553). safeJSONStorage (storage.ts:43-58) writes to window.localStorage with no encryption/obfuscation. Any XSS, malicious browser extension, or shared-machine access reads the key verbatim. GeminiSettingsModal.tsx has no warning text about local plaintext storage (grep for 'plaintext|localStorage|not secure' returns nothing). This is a user-billing-cost exposure (the key is billable).
fix: Product decision needed: either (a) add a one-line caution under the API key input in GeminiSettingsModal.tsx ('Your key is stored unencrypted in this browser only and never sent to our servers'), or (b) move geminiApiKey to sessionStorage (cleared on tab close) by giving the persist config a separate storage, or (c) drop geminiApiKey from partialize so it lives only in memory for the session. Each changes UX/persistence behavior.
_area: L2 Security · Key handling + sanitize wiring + scaffolding

title: secureFetch() exported but never used; RESTAdapter uses raw fetch instead
detail: secureFetch() (rate limiting, CSRF header injection, timeout, same-origin credentials) is defined here and re-exported via utils/index.ts:136, but has ZERO consumers project-wide (grep across src/ minus the def+barrel returns nothing). The actual SCADA caller src/scada/adapters/RESTAdapter.ts:332 uses raw `fetch(url, { ...options, signal })` and manually sets `Authorization: Bearer ${this.config.apiKey}` at line 354 rather than secureFetch. So the 'secure' wrapper is dead scaffolding.
fix: Either delete secureFetch + its SecureFetchOptions type and remove from the barrel (dead-code cleanup), or have RESTAdapter adopt it. For launch, removing the unused export is the low-risk path; adopting it in RESTAdapter changes runtime request behavior and needs an owner call.
_area: L2 Security · Key handling + sanitize wiring + scaffolding

title: CSRF helpers (generate/getOrCreate/clearCsrfToken) exported but never used — dead scaffolding
detail: generateCsrfToken (344), getOrCreateCsrfToken (361), clearCsrfToken (375) are only consumed internally by secureFetch (line 553), which itself has no callers. Grep for these three across src/ (minus def+barrel) returns nothing. With no backend/server auth (frontend-only GitHub Pages app), client-side CSRF tokens validate against nothing. They are dead exports re-exported via index.ts:132-134.
fix: Remove the three CSRF exports from utils/index.ts and delete the unused functions (and CSRF_TOKEN_KEY) once secureFetch is also removed. Pure dead-code deletion; no runtime behavior depends on them.
_area: L2 Security · Key handling + sanitize wiring + scaffolding

title: JWT helpers (decodeTokenPayload/isTokenExpired/getTokenExpiresIn) exported but never used
detail: decodeTokenPayload (268), isTokenExpired (306), getTokenExpiresIn (323) and TokenPayload type are 'for future auth integration' (per file header) but have ZERO consumers — grep across src/ minus def+barrel returns nothing. The app has no auth/JWT flow (frontend-only, no backend). Re-exported via index.ts:129-131. Dead scaffolding shipping to production.
fix: Remove these three exports + TokenPayload from utils/index.ts and delete the unused functions, or move to a clearly-marked future/ stub. Dead-code cleanup, no runtime impact.
_area: L2 Security · Key handling + sanitize wiring + scaffolding

title: rateLimiters (gemini/chat/connect) exported but never wired — no rate limiting active
detail: rateLimiters.gemini/.chat/.connect are pre-built wrappers intended to throttle Gemini calls, chat spam, and reconnection storms, but grep for `rateLimiters.` across src/ (minus def+barrel) returns nothing. So untrusted-peer chat (sendChat at MultiplayerManager.ts:596) has no spam limit and Gemini calls have no client-side quota guard despite the wrappers existing. checkRateLimit itself is also unused outside this file.
fix: Wire rateLimiters.chat around sendChat() in MultiplayerManager.ts and rateLimiters.gemini around geminiClient.generateContent calls, OR remove the unused exports. Wiring changes runtime behavior (messages/requests can now be dropped) so it needs an owner decision; the removal path is the safe launch option.
_area: L2 Security · Key handling + sanitize wiring + scaffolding

title: maskSensitiveData / clearSensitiveFields exported but never used; key masking done ad hoc
detail: maskSensitiveData (612) and clearSensitiveFields (633) are exported via index.ts:137-138 but have ZERO consumers. GeminiSettingsModal.tsx:237 displays the key via geminiClient.getMaskedApiKey() (geminiClient.ts:165), which re-implements masking inline (`slice(0,4)...slice(-4)`) instead of using the shared maskSensitiveData. Duplicated logic + dead export.
fix: Either have getMaskedApiKey() delegate to maskSensitiveData (de-dupe), or remove maskSensitiveData/clearSensitiveFields from the barrel as dead code. Consolidation is low-risk but touches geminiClient output format slightly; removal is cleanest for launch.
_area: L2 Security · Key handling + sanitize wiring + scaffolding

title: sanitizeWorkerName / sanitizeUrl / isUrlSafe / decodeHtmlEntities exported but never used
detail: sanitizeWorkerName (224), sanitizeUrl (254), isUrlSafe (287), decodeHtmlEntities (57), validateObject/createStringValidator/createNumberValidator are exported via index.ts:99-115 but grep across src/ (minus def+barrel) returns ZERO consumers. These are unused sanitization scaffolding shipped to the bundle. Not a vulnerability by itself but inflates bundle and signals unfinished security wiring.
fix: After wiring the chat/name sanitizers (the genuinely needed ones), prune the remaining unused sanitize exports from utils/index.ts or wire sanitizeWorkerName where custom worker names are accepted. Dead-code cleanup, no runtime impact.
_area: L2 Security · Key handling + sanitize wiring + scaffolding

title: Misleading log: 'Initialized with Gemini 2.0 Flash' while model is 'gemini-3-flash-preview'
detail: Line 135 configures `model: 'gemini-3-flash-preview'` but the success log at line 146 says `logger.info('[GeminiClient] Initialized with Gemini 2.0 Flash')`. Mislabeled diagnostic; not a security hole but degrades incident-response accuracy when reading logs about which model handled a request. Pricing constants in aiConfigStore.ts:26-27 are labeled 'Gemini Flash ... as of Dec 2024' which also may not match gemini-3 pricing — flagging the log-string mismatch as the concrete, citable issue.
fix: Change the log string at line 146 to match the actual model id, e.g. `logger.info('[GeminiClient] Initialized with gemini-3-flash-preview')`.
_area: L2 Security · Key handling + sanitize wiring + scaffolding

title: API key retained on GeminiClient instance and never cleared from response cache scope
detail: initialize() stores `this.apiKey = apiKey` (line 130). disconnect() nulls it (line 177), which is correct. The key is never logged (getMaskedApiKey masks correctly), so no leak via logger. The residual concern is only that the in-memory plaintext key plus the persisted localStorage copy (aiConfigStore.ts:558) means two live plaintext copies; clearSensitiveFields() exists for exactly this but is unused. Lower confidence because JS gives no real memory-zeroing guarantee anyway and disconnect already nulls the reference.
fix: No code change strictly required; if hardening, route key clearing through clearSensitiveFields in disconnect() for consistency. Primarily reinforces the plaintext-localStorage finding above — fix that one first.
_area: L2 Security · Key handling + sanitize wiring + scaffolding

title: RESTAdapter sets Bearer token via raw fetch, bypassing secureFetch timeout/validation wrapper
detail: Line 354 `headers.set('Authorization', 'Bearer ${this.config.apiKey}')` is sent via raw fetch at line 332 (`return await fetch(url, { ...options, signal: controller.signal })`). The codebase ships a secureFetch wrapper (apiSecurity.ts:512) with timeout + same-origin credentials + audit hooks specifically for this, but RESTAdapter does not use it. The Bearer key (SCADA endpoint credential) rides on whatever URL config.url points to with no allowed-host validation. Confirms the secureFetch dead-code finding from the consumer side.
fix: Owner decision: either standardize RESTAdapter on secureFetch (gains timeout/audit, but changes request semantics), or document that raw fetch is intentional and remove the unused secureFetch. Adapter behavior change needs validation against the 1199-test baseline.
_area: L2 Security · Key handling + sanitize wiring + scaffolding
